** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''杜本神社'''（もりもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。同名神社として{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}・{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}に1社ずつがあり、いずれも{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|安宿郡}}の{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）「杜本神社二座」の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。羽曳野市の杜本神社の方が有力な論社とされる。羽曳野市の杜本神社は縁切り縁結びや命名、占いに御利益があるとされている。 ===概要=== どちらも祭神に{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}が含まれている。一説には、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}の時代、経津主命の14世の孫の伊波別命（いわわきのみこと）が、祖神・経津主神の陵墓のある地に住み、経津主神を祀ったのが起源であるという。 延喜式神名帳に名神大社として記されるほか、延喜式で当社の祭礼が「杜本祭」として記載され、内蔵寮の幣使が参向していた。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の{{jalink|高屋城|Q11669272}}攻めの際、兵火によって社殿・社宝を失い、{{jalink|1586年|Q6739}}（天正14年）、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550}}に社領を没収され衰微した。 === 杜本神社 (羽曳野市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}駒ケ谷64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}}
経津主姫命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月8日|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== 経津主命と経津主姫命の夫婦神を祀るとしているが、『神社明細帳』では{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}・経津主命、『河内国式神私考』では{{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|句々廼智命}}・{{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|氷谷坐彌豆波乃売神}}、『地名辞典』『神祇志料』『地理志料』では「当宗忌寸の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}」としている。 ===歴史=== 創建年代は不詳である。社伝によると、伊波別命に縁の一族が神社の祭祀を行い、平安時代には「矢作忌寸」を称したという。{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}時代に{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の金剛輪寺が創建され、{{jalink|社僧}}により奉仕されたが、明治の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により金剛輪寺は廃寺となった。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列格し、明治41年（{{jalink|1908年|Q2056}}）に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}となった。 === 境内 === 境内の本殿前左右には、「隼人石」と呼ばれる石造物1対がある。{{jalink|獣頭人身}}像が彫られたもので、造立年代は不明だが、{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}の「那富山墓」にある{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}}第1石によく似る{{Cite web|和書|title=「隼人石」|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=羽曳野市|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja|last=羽曳野市}}。 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって左） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって右） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|金剛輪寺（旧神宮寺） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|伝楠木正成首塚 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|伝藤原永手墓碑 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 現在の社殿は江戸時代に{{jalink|高津宮|Q11671792}}から移築したものである。境内社として以下のものがある。 *維日谷稚宮（若宮神社） - 祭神：{{jalink|反正天皇|Q329769}}・伊波別命・遠登売命。 *南木神社 - 祭神：正一位橘朝臣{{jalink|楠木正成|Q382234}}公。 *天満宮 *天照皇太神社 *光国稲荷神社 *光吉稲荷神社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 文化財 === ==== 羽曳野市指定文化財 ==== * 有形文化財 ** 阿闍梨覚峰関係資料（歴史資料） - 2013年（平成25年）4月25日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の羽曳野市ファイルより。。 === 杜本神社 (柏原市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
社殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}国分東条町 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|無格社}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} 祭神は経津主命一座のみであり、『明治神社明細帳』では「祭神不詳」と記されている。 創立年代は不詳である。明治時代の社格は{{jalink|無格社}}であった。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）12月25日、{{jalink|国分神社}}（柏原市国分市場）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され、その境内社となった。昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）3月、旧地に社殿が再興された。国分神社内の境内社（森本神社）も現存している。 File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|{{jalink|国分神社}}境内の森本神社 ===脚注=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|杜本神社 (羽曳野市)}} *{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''杜本神社'''（もりもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。同名神社として{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}・{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}に1社ずつがあり、いずれも{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|安宿郡}}の{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）「杜本神社二座」の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。羽曳野市の杜本神社の方が有力な論社とされる。羽曳野市の杜本神社は縁切り縁結びや命名、占いに御利益があるとされている。 ===概要=== どちらも祭神に{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}が含まれている。一説には、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}の時代、経津主命の14世の孫の伊波別命（いわわきのみこと）が、祖神・経津主神の陵墓のある地に住み、経津主神を祀ったのが起源であるという。 延喜式神名帳に名神大社として記されるほか、延喜式で当社の祭礼が「杜本祭」として記載され、内蔵寮の幣使が参向していた。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の{{jalink|高屋城|Q11669272}}攻めの際、兵火によって社殿・社宝を失い、{{jalink|1586年|Q6739}}（天正14年）、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550}}に社領を没収され衰微した。 === 杜本神社 (羽曳野市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}駒ケ谷64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}}
経津主姫命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月8日|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== 経津主命と経津主姫命の夫婦神を祀るとしているが、『神社明細帳』では{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}・経津主命、『河内国式神私考』では{{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|句々廼智命}}・{{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|氷谷坐彌豆波乃売神}}、『地名辞典』『神祇志料』『地理志料』では「当宗忌寸の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}」としている。 ===歴史=== 創建年代は不詳である。社伝によると、伊波別命に縁の一族が神社の祭祀を行い、平安時代には「矢作忌寸」を称したという。{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}時代に{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の金剛輪寺が創建され、{{jalink|社僧}}により奉仕されたが、明治の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により金剛輪寺は廃寺となった。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列格し、明治41年（{{jalink|1908年|Q2056}}）に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}となった。 === 境内 === 境内の本殿前左右には、「隼人石」と呼ばれる石造物1対がある。{{jalink|獣頭人身}}像が彫られたもので、造立年代は不明だが、{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}の「那富山墓」にある{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}}第1石によく似る{{Cite web|和書|title=「隼人石」|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=羽曳野市|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja|last=羽曳野市}}。 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって左） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって右） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|金剛輪寺（旧神宮寺） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|伝楠木正成首塚 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|伝藤原永手墓碑 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 現在の社殿は江戸時代に{{jalink|高津宮|Q11671792}}から移築したものである。境内社として以下のものがある。 *維日谷稚宮（若宮神社） - 祭神：{{jalink|反正天皇|Q329769}}・伊波別命・遠登売命。 *南木神社 - 祭神：正一位橘朝臣{{jalink|楠木正成|Q382234}}公。 *天満宮 *天照皇太神社 *光国稲荷神社 *光吉稲荷神社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 文化財 === ==== 羽曳野市指定文化財 ==== * 有形文化財 ** 阿闍梨覚峰関係資料（歴史資料） - 2013年（平成25年）4月25日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の羽曳野市ファイルより。。 === 杜本神社 (柏原市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
社殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}国分東条町 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|無格社}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} 祭神は経津主命一座のみであり、『明治神社明細帳』では「祭神不詳」と記されている。 創立年代は不詳である。明治時代の社格は{{jalink|無格社}}であった。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）12月25日、{{jalink|国分神社}}（柏原市国分市場）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され、その境内社となった。昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）3月、旧地に社殿が再興された。国分神社内の境内社（森本神社）も現存している。 File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|{{jalink|国分神社}}境内の森本神社 ===脚注=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|杜本神社 (羽曳野市)}} *{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''杜本神社'''（もりもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。同名神社として{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}・{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}に1社ずつがあり、いずれも{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|安宿郡}}の{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）「杜本神社二座」の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。羽曳野市の杜本神社の方が有力な論社とされる。羽曳野市の杜本神社は縁切り縁結びや命名、占いに御利益があるとされている。 ===概要=== どちらも祭神に{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}が含まれている。一説には、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}の時代、経津主命の14世の孫の伊波別命（いわわきのみこと）が、祖神・経津主神の陵墓のある地に住み、経津主神を祀ったのが起源であるという。 延喜式神名帳に名神大社として記されるほか、延喜式で当社の祭礼が「杜本祭」として記載され、内蔵寮の幣使が参向していた。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の{{jalink|高屋城|Q11669272}}攻めの際、兵火によって社殿・社宝を失い、{{jalink|1586年|Q6739}}（天正14年）、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550}}に社領を没収され衰微した。 === 杜本神社 (羽曳野市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}駒ケ谷64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}}
経津主姫命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月8日|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== 経津主命と経津主姫命の夫婦神を祀るとしているが、『神社明細帳』では{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}・経津主命、『河内国式神私考』では{{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|句々廼智命}}・{{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|氷谷坐彌豆波乃売神}}、『地名辞典』『神祇志料』『地理志料』では「当宗忌寸の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}」としている。 ===歴史=== 創建年代は不詳である。社伝によると、伊波別命に縁の一族が神社の祭祀を行い、平安時代には「矢作忌寸」を称したという。{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}時代に{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の金剛輪寺が創建され、{{jalink|社僧}}により奉仕されたが、明治の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により金剛輪寺は廃寺となった。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列格し、明治41年（{{jalink|1908年|Q2056}}）に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}となった。 === 境内 === 境内の本殿前左右には、「隼人石」と呼ばれる石造物1対がある。{{jalink|獣頭人身}}像が彫られたもので、造立年代は不明だが、{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}の「那富山墓」にある{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}}第1石によく似る{{Cite web|和書|title=「隼人石」|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=羽曳野市|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja|last=羽曳野市}}。 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって左） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|隼人石（本殿向かって右） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|金剛輪寺（旧神宮寺） File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|伝楠木正成首塚 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|伝藤原永手墓碑 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 現在の社殿は江戸時代に{{jalink|高津宮|Q11671792}}から移築したものである。境内社として以下のものがある。 *維日谷稚宮（若宮神社） - 祭神：{{jalink|反正天皇|Q329769}}・伊波別命・遠登売命。 *南木神社 - 祭神：正一位橘朝臣{{jalink|楠木正成|Q382234}}公。 *天満宮 *天照皇太神社 *光国稲荷神社 *光吉稲荷神社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 文化財 === ==== 羽曳野市指定文化財 ==== * 有形文化財 ** 阿闍梨覚峰関係資料（歴史資料） - 2013年（平成25年）4月25日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の羽曳野市ファイルより。。 === 杜本神社 (柏原市) === {{神社 | 名称=杜本神社 | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
社殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}国分東条町 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|経津主命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|無格社}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=杜本神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} 祭神は経津主命一座のみであり、『明治神社明細帳』では「祭神不詳」と記されている。 創立年代は不詳である。明治時代の社格は{{jalink|無格社}}であった。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）12月25日、{{jalink|国分神社}}（柏原市国分市場）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され、その境内社となった。昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）3月、旧地に社殿が再興された。国分神社内の境内社（森本神社）も現存している。 File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|{{jalink|国分神社}}境内の森本神社 ===脚注=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|杜本神社 (羽曳野市)}} *{{jalink|隼人石|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} and {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}; both are {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}) of {{jalink|Asuka District}} in {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era, when {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} attacked {{jalink|Takaya Castle|Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{jalink|1586|Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 8|Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{jalink|shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} in Meiji 41 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{jalink|Kozu-gu|Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Hanzei|Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{jalink|Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{jalink|Unranked Shrine}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was merged into {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{jalink|Kokubu Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{jalink|Hayato stones|Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill||lt=Asuka District|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill||lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill||lt=Unranked Shrine|UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill||lt=Unranked Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill||lt=Kokubu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill||lt=Kokubu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
