category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111|1=Morimotono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272|1=Takaya Castle}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792|1=Kōzu-gū}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769|1=Emperor Hanzei}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379|1=Hayato stones}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * もりもとじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Morimotono Shrine|lt=Morimotono Shrine|WD=Q135039111|1=Morimotono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ケ谷64', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市駒ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:杜本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523595}} == Japanese content == '''Morimoto Shrine''' (もりもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. There is one shrine of this name each in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} and {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}; both are {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of "Morimoto Shrine, two seats," a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asuka District|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}. The Habikino Morimoto Shrine is considered the stronger candidate. It is said to grant blessings for cutting/enjoining relationships, naming, and divination. === Overview === Both include {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} among their enshrined deities. One theory says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, 14th-generation descendant of Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, lived at the place of Futsunushi's tomb and enshrined the ancestral deity there. Besides being recorded as a myojin taisha in the Engishiki Jinmyocho, its festival is listed in the Engishiki as "Morimoto Festival," and emissaries from the Treasury Bureau attended. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} attacked {{ill|lt=Takaya Castle|Q11669272|WD=Q11669272|1=Takaya Castle}}, shrine buildings and treasures were lost in war fire; in {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} (Tensho 14), lands were confiscated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, causing decline. === Morimoto Shrine (Habikino) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}Komagatani 64 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44|N|135|37|33|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === It is said to enshrine the married pair Futsunushi-no-Mikoto and Futsunushi-hime-no-Mikoto, but ''Shrine Register'' records {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto; ''Kawachi no Kuni Shikijin Shiko'' lists {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} and {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Hiyatani-ni-imasu Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}}; ''Place Name Dictionary,'' ''Jingi Shiryo,'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' call it "the ancestral deity of the Tosoki no Sukune clan." === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says the clan related to Iwawakino-Mikoto conducted rites, and in the Heian period called themselves "Yahagi Imiki." In the era of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shugo|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, shrine-temple Kongorin-ji was founded and served by shrine monks, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in Meiji. It was ranked village shrine in Meiji 5 (1872), and became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}}). === Precincts === In front of the main hall stand a pair of stone objects called "Hayato stones." They are beast-head human-body carvings; date unknown, but closely resemble the first Hayato stone at "Nafuyama Tomb" in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}{{Cite web|和書|title="Hayato Stone"|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kaisetsu/2466.html|website=Habikino City|accessdate=2019-10-04|language=ja}}. File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-1.jpg|Hayato stone (left facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Hayatoishi-2.jpg|Hayato stone (right facing main hall) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kongorin-ji.jpg|Kongorin-ji (former shrine-temple) File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Kubizuka of Kusunoki Masashige.jpg|Traditional grave mound of Kusunoki Masashige's head File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), Tomb of Fujiwara-no-Nagate.jpg|Traditional gravestone of Fujiwara no Nagate File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === The current shrine building was moved in from {{ill|lt=Kozu-gu|Q11671792|WD=Q11671792|1=Kōzu-gū}} in the Edo period. Precinct shrines include: * Idaya Wakamiya (Wakamiya Shrine) - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Hanzei|Q329769|WD=Q329769|1=Emperor Hanzei}}, Iwawakino-Mikoto, Otome-no-Mikoto. * Nanki Shrine - deity: Senior First Rank Tachibana no Ason {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. * Tenmangu * Amateru Kotai Shrine * Mitsukuni Inari Shrine * Mitsuyoshi Inari Shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === ==== Habikino City designated cultural properties ==== * Tangible cultural property ** Materials related to Ajari Kakuhō (historical materials) - designated April 25, 2013 (Heisei 25)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Habikino City file, updated Nov 21, 2018).. === Morimoto Shrine (Kashiwara) === {{神社 | 名称=Morimoto Shrine | 画像=[[File:Morimoto-jinja (Kashiwara), shaden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}Kokubu Tojo-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} 2 seats) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Morimoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01.3|N|135|39|30|E}}}} }} Only one deity, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto, is enshrined; however, ''Meiji Shrine Register'' states "deity unknown." The founding date is unknown. Its Meiji-era rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was merged into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokubu Ichiba, Kashiwara) and became a precinct shrine there. In March Showa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former site. A precinct shrine (Morimoto Shrine) inside Kokubu Shrine still exists. File:Kokubu-jinja (Kashiwara), Morimoto-jinja.jpg|Morimoto Shrine in the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokubu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Morimoto-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|Morimoto Shrine (Habikino)}} *{{ill|lt=Hayato stones|Q85884379|WD=Q85884379|1=Hayato stones}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Morimoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|名もりもとしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧無格社]][[Category:qq]]
